sanskrit:
ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं परिज्ञाता त्रिविधा कर्मचोदना ।
करणं कर्म कर्तेति त्रिविधः कर्मसंग्रहः ॥
transliteration:
jñānaṃ jñeyaṃ parijñātā trividhā karmacodanā |
karaṇaṃ karma karteti trividhaḥ karmasaṅgrahaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Knowledge, the knowable, and the knower are the threefold impetus to action; the instruments, the action, and the agent are the threefold constituents of action.
contextual_meaning: Every human act is a mini-drama with six cast-members: the idea (knowledge), the goal (object), the self (knower), the tools (senses), the deed, and the doer.  Recognizing these roles loosens the ego’s grip and turns action into mindful play rather than anxious ownership.
psychological_state: Cognitive defusion—observing the components of action without fusing with any of them; metacognitive awareness replaces over-identification.
trigger_scenario: You’re about to hit 'send' on a heated email.  Noticing 'I have a thought (knowledge), a goal (object), a body (instruments), and a finger (agent)' lets you pause, breathe, and choose differently.
anchor_text: Notice the six actors on your inner stage and let the play unfold without clinging.
